intro: New Zealand holiday parks are the best-value family accommodation option — kitchen facilities, playgrounds, pool access, and a community feel. Here are the best family-friendly holiday parks across NZ, by region.

parks.name: Paihia Beach Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Beach access, Bay of Islands cruises
parks.description: A waterfront family base in the Bay of Islands, walking distance to the ferry terminal and cruise departures. Clean facilities and a good playground make it an easy stop for families exploring Northland's beaches and dolphin tours.

parks.name: Paihia TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Budget families, central location
parks.description: A central, budget-friendly TOP 10 park in the heart of Paihia. With a pool, camp kitchen and town on your doorstep, it's a reliable choice for families touring the Bay of Islands on a budget.

parks.name: Matauri Bay Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Secluded beach, snorkelling
parks.description: A secluded, affordable park right on a stunning Northland beach with snorkelling off the sand. Quiet and relaxed, it suits families wanting to escape the crowds and spend days in the water.

parks.name: Hot Water Beach TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Hot Water Beach access, natural hot pools
parks.description: Walk straight to the famous Hot Water Beach where you can dig your own thermal spa pool at low tide. An excellent pool complex, camp shop and summer entertainment make this a standout Coromandel family park.

parks.name: Hahei Beach Accommodation
parks.best_for: Cathedral Cove access, boutique feel
highlights: 5 min walk to Cathedral Cove track
amenities: 5 min walk to Cathedral Cove track
parks.description: A boutique-feel base just five minutes' walk from the Cathedral Cove track. Set in a quiet beach village, it's best for families with older kids who want to explore the Coromandel's most famous coastline.

parks.name: Whitianga Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Central Coromandel base, ferry to Cathedral Cove
parks.description: A central Coromandel base near the Whitianga ferry wharf, with family cabins and good facilities. A practical choice for reaching Cathedral Cove and the wider Mercury Bay area.

parks.name: Rotorua TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Central Rotorua base, geothermal pools on-site
parks.description: A central Rotorua park with its own natural geothermal spa pool, a five-minute walk from town. A great playground and camp kitchen make it an easy family base for exploring Rotorua's geysers and Redwoods.

parks.name: Taupo DeBretts Spa Resort
parks.best_for: Thermal pools, family spa experience
parks.description: A family spa experience with massive thermal pools and a waterslide included in your stay. Private thermal pools and good cabins make this a memorable, slightly higher-end Taupo stay.

parks.name: All Seasons Holiday Park Taupo
parks.best_for: Budget families in Taupo
parks.description: A budget-friendly Taupo park with lake views and good kitchen facilities. An affordable base for families exploring Lake Taupo, Huka Falls and the central plateau.

parks.name: Hutt Park Holiday Cottages
parks.best_for: Wellington city proximity, large park
parks.description: A large, well-equipped holiday park close to Wellington city, with a playground and good facilities. A handy base for families visiting Te Papa, the cable car and the capital's attractions.

parks.name: Paraparaumu Beach Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Beach access, Kapiti Island views
parks.description: A quiet Kapiti Coast park with beach access and views across to Kapiti Island. Great for younger kids, with safe swimming and an easy day trip to the island bird sanctuary.

parks.name: Nelson TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Abel Tasman gateway, sunniest region in NZ
highlights: Close to Abel Tasman National Park
amenities: Close to Abel Tasman National Park
parks.description: The gateway to Abel Tasman National Park in NZ's sunniest region. With a pool, good cabins and full TOP 10 benefits, it's a comfortable family base for kayaking and golden-sand beaches.

parks.name: Kaiteriteri Beach Motor Camp
parks.best_for: Abel Tasman National Park, golden sand beach
highlights: Abel Tasman water taxis depart here
amenities: Abel Tasman water taxis depart here
parks.description: Right on golden Kaiteriteri Beach where the Abel Tasman water taxis depart. The summer atmosphere is buzzing and sites book out months ahead, so plan early for a beachfront family holiday.

parks.name: Picton TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Marlborough Sounds base, ferry terminal proximity
parks.description: A clean, well-run park within walking distance of the Picton ferry terminal and Marlborough Sounds. An ideal first or last stop for families crossing Cook Strait.

parks.name: Christchurch TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: City base, heated pool, close to Hagley Park
parks.description: A city base with a heated indoor pool, great playground and a range of cabins and units. Walking distance to attractions and close to Hagley Park, it's a comfortable Canterbury family stay.

parks.name: Hokitika Holiday Park
parks.best_for: West Coast base, greenstone and glowworm caves nearby
parks.description: A quiet, affordable West Coast base with a walk to the beach and easy access to greenstone shopping and glowworm caves. A good overnight stop on the way to the glaciers.

parks.name: Franz Josef TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Glacier access, West Coast adventure
parks.description: Walking distance to the Franz Josef glacier walks with an on-site hot pool to soak in afterwards. Affordable for the glacier region and a solid base for West Coast adventures.

parks.name: Queenstown Lakeview TOP 10
parks.best_for: Lake and mountain views, close to everything
parks.description: A walk-to-town Queenstown park with lake and mountain views from many sites. Central and family-friendly, it puts gondola rides, lake cruises and adventure activities within easy reach.

parks.name: Creeksyde TOP 10 Queenstown
parks.best_for: Quieter location, more affordable Queenstown base
parks.description: Creeksyde TOP 10 Queenstown in Queenstown — best for quieter location, more affordable queenstown base. A family-friendly holiday park with peaceful creekside setting, short drive to town, good value for queenstown.

parks.name: Wanaka TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Lake Wanaka access, Puzzling World nearby
parks.description: Wanaka TOP 10 Holiday Park in Wanaka — best for lake wanaka access, puzzling world nearby. A family-friendly holiday park with walk to lake wanaka, excellent facilities, good value vs queenstown.

parks.name: Te Anau TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Milford Sound gateway, Fiordland base
parks.description: Te Anau TOP 10 Holiday Park in Te Anau — best for milford sound gateway, fiordland base. A family-friendly holiday park with perfect milford sound base, lakefront location, well-run facilities.

parks.name: Invercargill TOP 10 Holiday Park
parks.best_for: Bluff gateway, Catlins road trip base
parks.description: Invercargill TOP 10 Holiday Park in Invercargill — best for bluff gateway, catlins road trip base. A family-friendly holiday park with good value, clean facilities, good for southbound road trips.

tips.title: Book early for school holidays
tips.body: The best holiday parks — especially at Hot Water Beach, Kaiteriteri, and Queenstown — book out 3–6 months ahead for school holiday periods. Don't leave it to the last month.

tips.title: TOP 10 membership saves 10%
tips.body: A TOP 10 annual membership costs around $49 and gives 10% off at all 50+ TOP 10 holiday parks. It pays for itself in 2–3 nights and is worth buying before any family NZ road trip.

tips.title: Cabins vs powered sites
tips.body: Most holiday parks offer powered sites (for campervans and tents) and cabins/units. Cabins with bathrooms (called 'tourist units' or 'ensuite cabins') sleep families for $120–$200/night, often much cheaper than motels with better facilities.

tips.title: Kitchen facilities cut your food budget
tips.body: Holiday park camp kitchens let you cook your own meals. A family of 4 can eat well for $30–$50/day vs $120+ for three restaurant meals. Most parks have BBQ areas too — stock up at supermarkets.

tips.title: Arrival time matters
tips.body: Most holiday parks have check-in from 2pm. Arriving late on a busy summer day can mean smaller or less sheltered sites. Call ahead if running late — they often hold your booking but may re-allocate spots.

faqs.q: What is a holiday park in New Zealand?
faqs.a: A New Zealand holiday park (also called a motor camp or campsite) is a multi-option accommodation facility offering powered sites for campervans and tents, basic cabins, and self-contained units — all typically sharing communal kitchen, bathroom, and laundry facilities. Most have playgrounds, BBQ areas, and some have pools. They're the best-value family accommodation in NZ.

faqs.q: Are TOP 10 holiday parks worth it?
faqs.a: Yes — especially for families doing a multi-night NZ road trip. TOP 10 parks are independently assessed and meet consistent quality standards. The TOP 10 membership card ($49/year) gives 10% off all stays at 50+ parks nationwide, paying for itself quickly. Not all great parks are TOP 10 parks, but TOP 10 membership is reliable indicator of quality.

faqs.q: How much does a holiday park cost per night in NZ?
faqs.a: Powered sites for campervans or tents cost $35–$55/night for two adults. Cabins (shared bathrooms) run $80–$130/night. Self-contained tourist units with private bathrooms cost $120–$220/night. School holiday and Christmas/New Year prices are typically 20–40% higher than off-peak.

faqs.q: Can you book NZ holiday parks online?
faqs.a: Yes — most NZ holiday parks can be booked directly on their own website, through TOP 10's website (top10.co.nz), or via Booking.com and Campable. Direct booking is usually cheapest. The TOP 10 website includes all 50+ TOP 10 parks and allows easy searching by region.
